danger_signals.visual_form: Dark, grotesque sprite designs with contrasting colors against background
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat requiring avoidance or combat

danger_signals.visual_form: Spike patterns, pit openings, and obstacle placement in level geometry
danger_signals.meaning: Instant-death or damage zones requiring precise platforming

danger_signals.signal: Dark color palette in level sections
danger_signals.visual_form: Shadows and low-contrast environments in certain stage areas

safety_signals.visual_form: Clearly defined solid ground with contrasting color to background
Visual flow and platform placement guiding player rightward/upward

faction_indicators.color_identity: Typically lighter/brighter sprite against dark backgrounds
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Grotesque creature form distinguishes protagonist from standard enemies
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Player occupies foreground; central screen positioning

faction_indicators.color_identity: Dark, contrasting sprite designs with supernatural aesthetic
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple creature types with distinct silhouettes and color patterns
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Scattered throughout levels; occupies same plane as player

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Brighter, less cluttered backgrounds with clearer platform visibility
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Lower density of enemy encounters; more forgiving platforming sections

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Darker atmospheric tones with increased visual clutter and obstacle density
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Higher enemy spawn rates; more complex enemy patterns; tighter platforming requirements

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Distinct arena design; change in background or lighting to signal special encounter
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy activity ceases or concentrates; arena becomes isolated space

environmental_storytelling.example: Grotesque creature designs and shadowy environments establish supernatural/hostile world state

environmental_storytelling.technique: Level progression through increasingly hostile terrain
environmental_storytelling.example: Visual escalation from manageable platforms to dense obstacle fields communicates deepening danger
Enemy variety suggests diverse threats within same hostile world; protagonist as 'beast' among beasts establishes moral ambiguity

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Visible on-screen movement patterns; predictable patrol routes telegraph approach
Increased movement speed; directional orientation toward player position

entity_behavioral_tells.visual_manifestation: Sprite damage state or visual flashing (if implemented)

notes: Source provided only disambiguation page. Detailed visual communication analysis limited by lack of gameplay documentation. Shadow of the Beast (1989, Reflections Interactive) is the parent title; Genesis version (1991) is likely a port. Dark fantasy aesthetic and sprite-based communication are typical of early 1990s platformer design. Visual manifestation of world state relies primarily on classic level design progression: brightness/clarity decreasing, obstacle density increasing, enemy variety expanding. Faction distinction minimal—binary protagonist vs. enemy opposition typical of era.
